inclusion_criteria: Original human studies including healthy individuals and/or patients with psychiatric diagnoses who receive a specified psychoplastogen (ketamine, esketamine, LSD, psilocybin, ayahuasca, DMT, MDMA, scopolamine, or rapastinel) via any route/dose
Include a comparator of baseline (pre-treatment) BDNF within subjects and/or a placebo or other control condition
Report peripheral blood BDNF protein concentrations measured in serum or plasma with sufficient data to extract pre–post change and/or between-group differences
Study designs may include randomized or nonrandomized clinical trials, crossover trials, or observational intervention studies that allow pre–post or control comparisons
exclusion_criteria: Nonhuman, in vitro, or ex vivo stimulation-only studies; studies reporting only genetic, transcriptomic (mRNA), or non-protein measures
Non-original publications (reviews, editorials), case reports/series without a comparator, or abstracts with insufficient extractable data
Studies that do not administer one of the specified psychoplastogens as a primary intervention or where co-interventions prevent isolating its effect
Outcomes not measuring BDNF in serum or plasma or lacking a baseline/control comparator (e.g., CSF/tissue-only measurements)
screening_criteria.description: Screening for human studies assessing the effect of specified psychoplastogens on peripheral (serum/plasma) BDNF with baseline or placebo/control comparison.
